  "weights": {

  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",

    "R1.3",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "When you say “make this stronger,” which focus do you want: (A) polish and tighten wording/flow without changing the claims (primarily R1.6), (B) sharpen and possibly reframe the core contribution and logic to present a clearer value proposition without adding new evidence (R1.1/R1.2), or (C) align and, if needed, temper the claims against the reported results (e.g., handling ‘first’ and deployment-gap assertions) with minor structural tweaks (R1.3, optionally R1.5)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Strengthening often requires sharpening the core contribution and gap framing above the sentence level.",
    "R1.2": "Ensuring the argument chain is tight makes the abstract read as more convincing without adding new evidence.",
    "R1.3": "Claim strength depends on whether the stated experiments support the assertions (e.g., reliability and ‘first’ claims).",
    "R1.6": "Concise, high-impact phrasing and flow are a primary lever for making a short abstract feel stronger.",
    "RX.1": "We should clearly indicate uncertainty where evidence is limited (e.g., ‘to our knowledge’).",
    "RX.2": "Avoiding overclaims—especially around ‘first’ or generality across markets—is critical when intensifying language.",
    "RX.3": "We must stay within the scope of the provided abstract and not introduce new results or external sources.",
    "RX.4": "Language should not encourage unsafe or premature deployment in power systems contexts.",
    "RX.5": "Each strengthened claim must remain traceable to statements and results already present in the text."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: When you say “make this stronger,” which focus do you want: (A) polish and tighten wording/flow without changing the claims (primarily R1.6), (B) sharpen and possibly reframe the core contribution and logic to present a clearer value proposition without adding new evidence (R1.1/R1.2), or (C) align and, if needed, temper the claims against the reported results (e.g., handling ‘first’ and deployment-gap assertions) with minor structural tweaks (R1.3, optionally R1.5)?

rationale.R1.1: Strengthening often requires sharpening the core contribution and gap framing above the sentence level.
rationale.R1.2: Ensuring the argument chain is tight makes the abstract read as more convincing without adding new evidence.
rationale.R1.3: Claim strength depends on whether the stated experiments support the assertions (e.g., reliability and ‘first’ claims).
rationale.R1.6: Concise, high-impact phrasing and flow are a primary lever for making a short abstract feel stronger.
rationale.RX.1: We should clearly indicate uncertainty where evidence is limited (e.g., ‘to our knowledge’).
rationale.RX.2: Avoiding overclaims—especially around ‘first’ or generality across markets—is critical when intensifying language.
rationale.RX.3: We must stay within the scope of the provided abstract and not introduce new results or external sources.
rationale.RX.4: Language should not encourage unsafe or premature deployment in power systems contexts.
rationale.RX.5: Each strengthened claim must remain traceable to statements and results already present in the text.
